answer:
In the *Manusmṛti*, the Sanskrit term *guṇa* (गुण) is employed with explicit technical precision drawn from Sāṃkhya philosophy, designating the three fundamental constituents (*sattva*, *rajas*, *tamas*) of the Self (*ātman*) and all manifested reality. These are not mere descriptive "qualities" or "attributes" in a generic sense (as English translations often render them), but dynamic, pervasive principles that bind the Great One (*mahān*) to beings, determine mental states, ethical propensities, actions (*karma*), and rebirths across cosmic cycles.[^1]

*Sattva* embodies knowledge, purity, tranquility, and virtue—yielding heavenly or divine births; *rajas*, passion, aversion, and worldly striving—leading to human or semi-divine states; *tamas*, ignorance, delusion, and inertia—resulting in subhuman or hellish forms.[^2] Whatever *guṇa* predominates "makes that embodied being predominantly of that *guṇa*," shaping perception, conduct (e.g., *sāttvic* actions like Vedic study promote fame and Dharma; *tāmasic* ones like cruelty lead to bestial wombs), and ultimate soteriological trajectory.[^3]

English terms like "quality" or "attribute" dilute this by evoking static properties (e.g., color or shape), stripping *guṇa*'s Sāṃkhya specificity: they are the evolutes of *prakṛti* (primordial nature), eternally intermingled yet hierarchically ordered (*sattva* > *rajas* > *tamas*), driving saṃsāra until transcended via knowledge (*jñāna*). The *Manusmṛti* thus warns: fix the mind on Dharma to favor *sattva*, lest *tamas* prevail.[^4]

No primary Sāṃkhya texts (e.g., *Sāṃkhyakārikā*) are in the corpus, but this integration in *Manusmṛti* 12 illustrates *guṇa*'s doctrinal weight across Dharmaśāstra.

[^1]: *Manusmṛti*, ch. 12, [para. p-074f6e](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/12/translation/#p-074f6e) (Mn_12.23–25).
[^2]: *Manusmṛti*, ch. 12, [para. p-074f6e](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/12/translation/#p-074f6e) (Mn_12.26–29, 31–38).
[^3]: *Manusmṛti*, ch. 12, [para. p-074f6e](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/12/translation/#p-074f6e) (Mn_12.24–25, 39–51).
[^4]: *Manusmṛti*, ch. 12, [whole chapter](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/12/translation/) (esp. Mn_12.23, 125).
